URL: https://spj.science.org/doi/10.34133/2020/4361703

SUMMARY:
Bismuth telluride is the dominant thermoelectric material for applications near room temperature due to its inherently low lattice thermal conductivity and high electronic weighted mobility [1].

enriched_points.expanded_content: Bismuth telluride is the leading thermoelectric material for near-room temperature applications, offering low thermal conductivity and high electronic mobility, making it ideal for energy conversion.
Lead telluride is favored for high-temperature thermoelectric applications due to its favorable thermoelectric properties, although it poses toxicity and environmental concerns for large-scale usage.
Silver selenide exhibits promising thermoelectric performance at elevated temperatures, but its stability and scalability are significant challenges for practical applications.
Traditional thermoelectric materials like Bi2Te3 and PbTe are limited by their temperature ranges, toxicity, and efficiency, necessitating the search for new materials with enhanced properties.
